kit.referenceExcerpt: Mitochondria are membrane-bound organelles found in most eukaryotic cells; they generate most of the cell's ATP through oxidative phosphorylation.
kit.modelAnswer: Mitochondria produce most of a eukaryotic cell's ATP via oxidative phosphorylation, which is why they are called the powerhouses of the cell.
kit.learningObjective: Explain the role of mitochondria in energy production
